context: Codebase is in a mature state with sprints 001-011 already delivered. Phases below describe REMAINING work to reach 100% coverage of the 104 capabilities. Capabilities marked 'complete' in gap-analysis.md are not re-scheduled. 'Implemented' capabilities only appear where specific acceptance criteria are outstanding.

phases.name: Stability & Foundations Finish
phases.description: Resolve blocking findings from the 2026-03-16 codebase review and ship foundations that unblock every downstream workstream: audit log, machine metrics store, session tags, and the risk classifier.
review_findings_resolved: HI-01 protocol schemas not in discriminatedUnion
HI-03 approval schemas missing from browser.ts

parallel_workstreams.name: Protocol cleanup
parallel_workstreams.notes: Fix HI-01, HI-03: add approval/workforce schemas to discriminated unions + browser.ts export

parallel_workstreams.name: Risk classifier + dashboard bug fixes
parallel_workstreams.notes: Also fix HI-04 replay lifecycle and HI-05 WS status binding

exit_criteria: All 4 review blockers resolved
audit_log table shipped and wired to mutation routes
machine_metrics time-series table shipped
sessions.tags column migration applied
risk classifier produces correct low/medium/high/critical on test fixtures

phases.name: Approvals & Notifications Completeness
phases.description: Close the gap on the approvals workflow: SDK long-poll bridge, three-way decisions with feedback, Approve-and-Remember, AskUserQuestion and MCP elicitation dialog UIs, notification batching/escalation, in-session banner.

parallel_workstreams.name: SDK canUseTool bridge
parallel_workstreams.notes: Long-polling with reconnection — depends on CAP-022 risk classifier from R1

exit_criteria: Headless SDK sessions route approvals through hub (no auto-deny)
Approve-and-Remember creates policy rule with provenance
AskUserQuestion and MCP elicitation dialogs reach acceptance criteria
Browser and ntfy.sh channels pass integration test
Notifications batch within a 5s window and escalate by risk

phases.name: Scheduler & Session Lifecycle Completion
phases.description: Fill out session lifecycle gaps: machine capabilities matching, scoring auto-scheduler, timeout + cost enforcement, retry with backoff, SDK session discovery, dual-stream parsing.

parallel_workstreams.name: Capabilities + scheduler
parallel_workstreams.notes: CAP-013 first, then CAP-014 depends on it

parallel_workstreams.name: Timeout + cost enforcement
parallel_workstreams.notes: Depends on CAP-068 (cost capture) — verify that dependency before starting

exit_criteria: Creating a session without machineId succeeds and the scheduler places it atomically
Sessions exceeding timeout or cost limit terminate with correct reason codes
Failed queue tasks retry per configured policy with exponential backoff
Session detail Events tab shows events sourced from dual-stream parser

phases.name: Cost Tracking & Budgets
phases.description: Finish the cost pipeline end-to-end: complete pricing formula, per-query budget enforcement, period thresholds with notifications, upfront token estimator, cost dashboard polish, telemetry export.
exit_criteria: Pricing formula matches SDK-reported costs within 1% on contract test
maxBudgetUsd enforced in SDK call sites and returns correct error subtype
50/75/90/100% notifications fire idempotently
Token estimator shows upfront cost in job launcher
OTel export reaches configured endpoint in test run

phases.name: Workforce Completeness
phases.description: Close gaps in the workforce platform: workspace TTL cleanup, pre/post-flight checks, batch launcher, workspace/git WS message wiring end-to-end, checks API lifecycle, batch launcher UI.

parallel_workstreams.name: Workspace/git protocol wiring
parallel_workstreams.notes: Unblocked by HI-01 fix in R1

exit_criteria: Stale workspaces reaped by TTL sweeper in e2e test
Pre/post-flight output appears in job detail
Batch launcher creates per-repo jobs with concurrency cap
Workspace/git WS events surface live in dashboard
Checks API reports in_progress → completed with conclusion

phases.name: GitHub Setup Wizard & Rotation
phases.description: Ship one-click GitHub App manifest flow, Tailscale Funnel provisioning, and harden credential rotation + PR lifecycle.
exit_criteria: Manifest flow completes end-to-end in test tailnet
Tokens auto-rotate via @octokit/auth-app LRU
Funnel webhook URL validated and signed requests accepted
PAT fallback surfaces limitations and enables polling mode

phases.name: Docker Sandbox Hardening
phases.description: Finish the auto-accept sandbox story: restricted Docker network with allowlist proxy, pre-pull on agent startup, WSL2 spawn strategy, container stats accuracy, security baseline completeness.

parallel_workstreams.name: Stats reporter accuracy
parallel_workstreams.notes: Implement CPU% delta math per research-cache GAP note

exit_criteria: Container can reach api.anthropic.com but not arbitrary hosts (e2e test)
Agent fails startup if claude-code image pull fails
WSL2 spawn strategy passes SpawnedProcess contract test
Stats stream reports correct cpuPercent + memoryMB + pids

phases.name: Deployment & Operations
phases.description: Finalize Tailscale sidecar deployment, HTTPS via Tailscale Serve, Docker secrets, SQLite backup strategy, recordings volume retention.
exit_criteria: docker compose up boots Hub reachable at https://<hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net
Secrets mount under /run/secrets with .gitignore enforced
SQLite backup script or Litestream sidecar validated in test environment
Recordings older than RECORDINGS_MAX_AGE_DAYS are swept

phases.name: Completeness Sweep & Docs
phases.description: Final polish pass: status indicator system sweep across all views, machine sparklines/conditions finish, competitive landscape doc, any residual acceptance criteria on 'implemented' capabilities.

parallel_workstreams.name: Completeness audit
parallel_workstreams.notes: Run blitz:completeness-gate and resolve any residual acceptance gaps on capabilities currently marked 'implemented' in gap-analysis.md

exit_criteria: Every capability at status=complete in a fresh Phase-2 re-run
Competitive doc linked from README
No P1 findings in a fresh blitz:sprint-review

critical_path: R1 (protocol cleanup + metrics + audit + risk classifier) ->
R2 (approvals workflow depends on risk classifier + notification foundations) ->
R3 (session lifecycle needs metrics for scheduler) ->
R4 (cost tracking depends on R2 notification engine) ->
R5 (workforce completion unblocked by R1 protocol cleanup) ->
R6 (GitHub wizard needs R5's PR lifecycle polish) ->
R7 (sandbox hardening depends on R3 lifecycle) ->
R8 (deployment finalization depends on R6 Funnel URL wiring) ->
parallelism_notes: R1 workstreams are fully independent — maximum parallelism.
R2, R5, R7 each support 3-4 concurrent workstreams; these are the best targets for multi-agent sprints.
R3 and R4 share dependencies on R1+R2 but can start in parallel with each other once both entry criteria are met.
R6 and R7 can run in parallel once R3 and R5 are done (different domains, no shared files).
entry_criteria_global: Current codebase passes type-check, lint, test, and build
All 4 codebase review blockers (HI-01, HI-03, HI-04, HI-05) scheduled in R1
stop_conditions: Any phase that cannot ship its exit criteria after two review cycles should be broken into smaller sub-phases and re-estimated.
If a capability fails Phase-2 re-assessment as 'not_started' after its owning phase completes, flag as a planning gap.
